title: AP U.S. History MCQ Practice Exam 02

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of selected Columbian Exchange effects.
Item or Process | Movement or Effect
Smallpox and measles | Spread from Europe to the Americas
Maize and potatoes | Spread from the Americas to Europe, Africa, and Asia
Horses | Spread from Europe to the Americas
Silver | Extracted in Spanish America and entered global trade
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: European contact produced only environmental changes within Europe.
choices.B: The Columbian Exchange created biological, economic, and cultural connections across the Atlantic world.
choices.C: Native American societies remained unaffected by Afro-Eurasian animals and diseases.
choices.D: Spanish colonization ended global trade in precious metals.

questions.correctAnswerText: The Columbian Exchange created biological, economic, and cultural connections across the Atlantic world.
questions.explanation: The table shows the movement of diseases, crops, animals, and silver across continents, demonstrating broad Atlantic and global connections.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of selected Columbian Exchange effects: smallpox and measles spread from Europe to the Americas; maize and potatoes spread from the Americas to Europe, Africa, and Asia; horses spread from Europe to the Americas; silver from Spanish America entered global trade. Which development most directly resulted from the disease patterns shown?

choices.A: The immediate end of Spanish colonial expansion
choices.B: The creation of equal diplomatic alliances between Spain and all Native nations
choices.C: A decline in European interest in American resources
choices.D: Severe population losses among many Native communities

questions.correctAnswerText: Severe population losses among many Native communities
questions.explanation: Diseases introduced from Europe caused catastrophic mortality among many Native peoples who lacked prior exposure.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of selected Columbian Exchange effects: smallpox and measles spread from Europe to the Americas; maize and potatoes spread from the Americas to Europe, Africa, and Asia; horses spread from Europe to the Americas; silver from Spanish America entered global trade. Which broader historical situation best explains the movement of silver shown in the table?

choices.A: Spanish colonization connected American mining labor systems to expanding global commerce.
choices.B: English Puritans established plantation slavery in New England.
choices.C: Native peoples controlled all European Atlantic shipping routes.
choices.D: The United States used industrial factories to dominate global trade.

questions.correctAnswerText: Spanish colonization connected American mining labor systems to expanding global commerce.
questions.explanation: Silver extraction in Spanish America linked coerced labor and colonial mining to global trade networks.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text comparison chart of English colonial regions, c. 1700.
Region | Labor and economy | Settlement pattern | Social features
Chesapeake | Tobacco, enslaved and indentured labor | Dispersed plantations | Planter elite grew stronger
New England | Mixed farming, trade, fishing | Towns and villages | Congregational communities
Middle Colonies | Grain, commerce, mixed labor | Diverse towns and farms | Religious and ethnic pluralism
Which conclusion is best supported by the chart?

choices.A: All English colonies developed identical labor systems and social structures.
choices.B: New England relied more heavily on large tobacco plantations than the Chesapeake.
choices.C: Regional differences shaped colonial economies, settlement, and social hierarchies.
choices.D: The Middle Colonies rejected commercial farming and urban trade.

questions.correctAnswerText: Regional differences shaped colonial economies, settlement, and social hierarchies.
questions.explanation: The chart contrasts plantation labor, town settlement, mixed farming, and pluralism across regions.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text comparison chart of English colonial regions, c. 1700. The Chesapeake is associated with tobacco and plantation labor, New England with mixed farming and commerce, and the Middle Colonies with grain, commerce, and diverse settlement. Which factor most directly contributed to the Chesapeake pattern?

choices.A: The profitability of staple-crop agriculture in a warmer climate
choices.B: The absence of European demand for tobacco
choices.C: The dominance of compact Puritan town settlement
choices.D: The prohibition of enslaved labor in all southern colonies

questions.correctAnswerText: The profitability of staple-crop agriculture in a warmer climate
questions.explanation: Tobacco cultivation in the Chesapeake depended on climate, land, export demand, and labor systems.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text comparison chart of English colonial regions, c. 1700. New England is described as having towns, villages, mixed farming, fishing, trade, and Congregational communities. Which evidence would most strengthen the chart’s description of New England?

choices.A: Records showing widespread rice plantations worked by enslaved labor
choices.B: Town records showing local church membership, schools, and communal land decisions
choices.C: Ship manifests showing that New England had no involvement in trade
choices.D: Royal decrees creating Catholic missions across New England

questions.correctAnswerText: Town records showing local church membership, schools, and communal land decisions
questions.explanation: These records would directly support the chart’s description of New England town life and Congregational communities.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text comparison chart of English colonial regions, c. 1700. The Middle Colonies are described as religiously and ethnically plural, with diverse towns and farms. Which context best explains this pattern?

choices.A: The complete absence of European migration to the region
choices.B: The region’s isolation from Atlantic commerce
choices.C: The immediate abolition of private property in the colonies
choices.D: Dutch, English, German, and other migrant communities shaped the region’s development.

questions.correctAnswerText: Dutch, English, German, and other migrant communities shaped the region’s development.
questions.explanation: The Middle Colonies were marked by ethnic and religious diversity due to varied European migration and earlier Dutch settlement.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated revolutionary pamphlet, 1776, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “A people long accustomed to local assemblies cannot be expected to surrender every right to ministers across the sea. Independence is not rebellion against order; it is the recovery of government by consent.” The pamphlet’s point of view is best explained by which situation?

choices.A: Patriots increasingly argued that monarchy and parliamentary authority threatened colonial rights.
choices.B: Loyalists successfully convinced most colonists to reject independence.
choices.C: The federal government used the Constitution to suppress political parties.
choices.D: Enslaved people received full citizenship rights in every state during the Revolution.

questions.correctAnswerText: Patriots increasingly argued that monarchy and parliamentary authority threatened colonial rights.
questions.explanation: The pamphlet’s defense of consent and independence reflects Patriot arguments in 1776.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated revolutionary pamphlet, 1776, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The pamphlet links independence to “government by consent.” Which idea most directly shaped this argument?

choices.A: Social Darwinism
choices.B: Containment
choices.C: Natural rights and popular sovereignty
choices.D: Mercantilist protection of imperial trade

questions.correctAnswerText: Natural rights and popular sovereignty
questions.explanation: “Government by consent” reflects Enlightenment and republican ideas about rights and popular sovereignty.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated revolutionary pamphlet, 1776, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The pamphlet presents independence as the recovery of self-government. Which broader Atlantic context helped make such arguments influential?

choices.A: The decline of Enlightenment political thought
choices.B: Enlightenment ideas about rights, sovereignty, and legitimate government
choices.C: The disappearance of print culture in British North America
choices.D: The end of all imperial warfare in the eighteenth century

questions.correctAnswerText: Enlightenment ideas about rights, sovereignty, and legitimate government
questions.explanation: Enlightenment political thought helped shape revolutionary arguments against inherited authority and empire.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated revolutionary pamphlet, 1776, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The pamphlet claims that independence would restore government by consent. Which argument would most directly complicate the pamphlet’s claim?

choices.A: Colonial assemblies existed before independence.
choices.B: Patriots criticized monarchy and Parliament.
choices.C: The Declaration of Independence appealed to natural rights.
choices.D: Many women, enslaved people, free Black people, and Native peoples remained excluded from political equality.

questions.correctAnswerText: Many women, enslaved people, free Black people, and Native peoples remained excluded from political equality.
questions.explanation: The pamphlet’s broad language of consent was limited in practice by exclusions based on race, gender, status, and sovereignty.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that the weakness of the Articles of Confederation became more visible when state and national governments struggled to address debt, interstate disputes, and unrest in the 1780s. Which evidence best supports this interpretation?

choices.A: The passage of the Dawes Act
choices.B: Shays’ Rebellion and Congress’s limited ability to raise revenue
choices.C: The creation of the Monroe Doctrine
choices.D: The New Deal expansion of federal relief programs

questions.correctAnswerText: Shays’ Rebellion and Congress’s limited ability to raise revenue
questions.explanation: Shays’ Rebellion and fiscal weakness exposed problems under the Articles of Confederation.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that the Articles of Confederation struggled with debt, interstate disputes, and unrest in the 1780s. Which economic situation contributed most directly to these tensions?

choices.A: Wartime debt, scarce currency, and disputes over taxation
choices.B: The discovery of gold in California
choices.C: The collapse of the plantation economy after the Civil War
choices.D: The rise of mass consumer credit in the 1920s

questions.correctAnswerText: Wartime debt, scarce currency, and disputes over taxation
questions.explanation: Postwar economic instability contributed to unrest and concerns about weak central authority.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Secondary-source interpretation. A historian argues that the Articles of Confederation struggled with debt, interstate disputes, and unrest in the 1780s. Which development most directly followed from concerns like those described?

choices.A: The immediate creation of a national income tax
choices.B: The permanent abolition of state governments
choices.C: The Philadelphia Convention and creation of a stronger federal framework
choices.D: The United States’ entry into World War I

questions.correctAnswerText: The Philadelphia Convention and creation of a stronger federal framework
questions.explanation: Concerns about the Articles helped lead to the Constitutional Convention and a stronger federal system.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated letter from a young textile worker in Lowell, Massachusetts, 1835, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “The bell fixes each hour of the day. Wages give me a measure of independence, yet the overseer’s rules reach from the mill floor into the boardinghouse.” Which development is best reflected in the letter?

choices.A: The decline of factory discipline in early industrial towns
choices.B: The expansion of plantation slavery into New England factories
choices.C: The replacement of wage labor by household subsistence production
choices.D: The growth of wage labor and factory discipline in the early industrial economy

questions.correctAnswerText: The growth of wage labor and factory discipline in the early industrial economy
questions.explanation: The letter describes wage work, factory bells, overseers, and boardinghouse regulation.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated letter from a young textile worker in Lowell, Massachusetts, 1835, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The writer notes that wages provide “a measure of independence” but factory rules extend into the boardinghouse. Which perspective is most directly represented?

choices.A: A young woman experiencing both opportunity and restriction in industrial wage work
choices.B: A plantation owner defending enslaved labor in the Deep South
choices.C: A railroad executive opposing immigration
choices.D: A Native leader negotiating removal treaties

questions.correctAnswerText: A young woman experiencing both opportunity and restriction in industrial wage work
questions.explanation: The source presents wages as independence while emphasizing restrictions imposed by mill discipline.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated letter from a young textile worker in Lowell, Massachusetts, 1835, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The letter describes factory schedules, wages, and boardinghouse rules. Which broader cultural change was associated with this development?

choices.A: The disappearance of gender expectations in the workplace
choices.B: New debates over women’s labor, respectability, and independence
choices.C: The end of wage work among young women
choices.D: The decline of textile production in the Northeast before 1820

questions.correctAnswerText: New debates over women’s labor, respectability, and independence
questions.explanation: Lowell-style factory work created new opportunities for some women while raising questions about gender roles and control.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated letter from a young textile worker in Lowell, Massachusetts, 1835, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. Which evidence from the letter best supports the claim that industrialization changed workers’ relationship to time?

choices.A: The reference to boardinghouse rules
choices.B: The mention of a young worker writing a letter
choices.C: The statement that wages gave the worker independence
choices.D: The statement that “the bell fixes each hour of the day”

questions.correctAnswerText: The statement that “the bell fixes each hour of the day”
questions.explanation: Factory bells imposed clock-based discipline, changing workers’ relationship to time.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, c. 1803–1820. The Louisiana Purchase doubles the claimed size of the United States. The Mississippi River and New Orleans are highlighted as key commercial routes. Several Native nations are shown within the purchased territory. Which context best explains why U.S. leaders valued the region?

choices.A: Access to western lands and control of trade routes along the Mississippi River
choices.B: A desire to end all commercial agriculture in the West
choices.C: A plan to return the region to French imperial control
choices.D: The discovery of large industrial steel centers in the Great Plains

questions.correctAnswerText: Access to western lands and control of trade routes along the Mississippi River
questions.explanation: The Mississippi River and New Orleans were crucial to western commerce and expansion.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, c. 1803–1820. The Louisiana Purchase doubles the claimed size of the United States, highlights New Orleans and the Mississippi River, and shows Native nations within the purchased territory. Which diplomatic circumstance made the purchase possible?

choices.A: Spain’s decision to join the United States as a state
choices.B: Britain’s peaceful surrender of Canada
choices.C: Mexico’s recognition of U.S. control over Texas
choices.D: France’s willingness to sell the territory during the Napoleonic era

questions.correctAnswerText: France’s willingness to sell the territory during the Napoleonic era
questions.explanation: Napoleon’s imperial priorities and financial needs helped make the Louisiana Purchase possible.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, c. 1803–1820. The Louisiana Purchase doubles the claimed size of the United States and includes Native nations within the territory. Which debate was intensified by the acquisition?

choices.A: Whether the United States should join NATO
choices.B: Whether the federal government had constitutional authority to acquire new territory
choices.C: Whether the colonies should boycott British goods
choices.D: Whether Reconstruction governments should be readmitted to Congress

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether the federal government had constitutional authority to acquire new territory
questions.explanation: The purchase raised constitutional questions about implied powers and territorial acquisition.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated newspaper editorial from Kansas Territory, 1856, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “Men arrive not to farm but to vote. Rifles travel with ballots, and each side claims that popular sovereignty alone can decide the future of this territory.” Which issue is most directly reflected in the editorial?

choices.A: Whether the United States should annex Hawaii
choices.B: Whether tariffs should fund internal improvements
choices.C: Whether slavery would expand into western territories
choices.D: Whether women should gain voting rights nationally

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether slavery would expand into western territories
questions.explanation: The editorial’s references to popular sovereignty, voting, and violence in Kansas reflect sectional conflict over slavery expansion.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated newspaper editorial from Kansas Territory, 1856, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The editorial says that men arrive “not to farm but to vote” and that “rifles travel with ballots.” The situation described was most directly caused by which policy?

choices.A: The Kansas-Nebraska Act’s use of popular sovereignty
choices.B: The Monroe Doctrine’s warning against European colonization
choices.C: The Indian Removal Act’s creation of western reservations
choices.D: The Social Security Act’s old-age insurance program

questions.correctAnswerText: The Kansas-Nebraska Act’s use of popular sovereignty
questions.explanation: The Kansas-Nebraska Act allowed settlers to decide slavery’s status, encouraging competing migration and violence.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated newspaper editorial from Kansas Territory, 1856, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. Which evidence from the editorial best supports the claim that democratic procedures were being undermined by sectional conflict?

choices.A: The reference to the future of the territory
choices.B: The mention of settlers arriving in Kansas
choices.C: The claim that each side supported popular sovereignty
choices.D: The statement that “rifles travel with ballots”

questions.correctAnswerText: The statement that “rifles travel with ballots”
questions.explanation: This phrase shows that violence accompanied voting, undermining democratic procedures.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated newspaper editorial from Kansas Territory, 1856, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The editorial describes violence, voting, and sectional conflict over slavery in Kansas. Which broader development did such conflict help accelerate?

choices.A: The end of all political parties before the Civil War
choices.B: The collapse of the Second Party System and the rise of the Republican Party
choices.C: The immediate readmission of former Confederate states
choices.D: The adoption of isolationism after World War I

questions.correctAnswerText: The collapse of the Second Party System and the rise of the Republican Party
questions.explanation: Conflict over Kansas and slavery expansion helped destroy older party alignments and strengthen the Republican Party.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described lithograph, c. 1863–1865. The image shows African American men in Union uniforms drilling near a camp. Women and children stand near a schoolhouse in the background. A banner reads “Freedom and Union.” Which historical situation is most directly reflected in the lithograph?

choices.A: The failure of African Americans to participate in the Civil War
choices.B: The immediate end of racial discrimination in the Union army
choices.C: Emancipation expanded African American claims to military service, education, and citizenship.
choices.D: Reconstruction governments had already guaranteed full equality in all states.

questions.correctAnswerText: Emancipation expanded African American claims to military service, education, and citizenship.
questions.explanation: The lithograph connects Black Union service with family, schooling, freedom, and citizenship claims.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described lithograph, c. 1863–1865. African American soldiers drill near a camp, women and children stand near a schoolhouse, and a banner reads “Freedom and Union.” Which element of the lithograph best supports the claim that the Civil War became a struggle over citizenship as well as union?

choices.A: African American soldiers are shown wearing Union uniforms.
choices.B: The image includes trees near a military camp.
choices.C: The lithograph is dated during the 1860s.
choices.D: A schoolhouse appears in the background.

questions.correctAnswerText: African American soldiers are shown wearing Union uniforms.
questions.explanation: Black military service powerfully supported claims to citizenship and national belonging.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described lithograph, c. 1863–1865. African American men in Union uniforms drill near a camp while women and children stand near a schoolhouse under a banner reading “Freedom and Union.” Which federal action most directly helped make the scene possible?

choices.A: The Fugitive Slave Act
choices.B: The Compromise of 1850
choices.C: The Dawes Severalty Act
choices.D: The Emancipation Proclamation and subsequent recruitment of Black soldiers

questions.correctAnswerText: The Emancipation Proclamation and subsequent recruitment of Black soldiers
questions.explanation: The Emancipation Proclamation transformed Union war aims and opened the way for large-scale Black enlistment.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described lithograph, c. 1863–1865. African American soldiers, families, and a schoolhouse appear under a “Freedom and Union” banner. Which broader cultural change is most directly suggested by the schoolhouse in the image?

choices.A: The end of African American interest in education after emancipation
choices.B: Freedpeople and their allies viewed education as central to freedom and community building.
choices.C: Southern states fully funded integrated public schools during the Civil War.
choices.D: The Union army prohibited literacy among formerly enslaved people.

questions.correctAnswerText: Freedpeople and their allies viewed education as central to freedom and community building.
questions.explanation: The schoolhouse symbolizes the importance many freedpeople placed on literacy, education, and autonomy.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of urban and immigrant change.
Indicator | 1870 | 1900
Foreign-born population in major industrial cities | large | larger
Tenement housing density | rising | very high
Factory and service jobs | expanding | expanded further
Municipal services | uneven | strained by rapid growth
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Immigration and industrial job growth contributed to rapid urbanization and pressure on city services.
choices.B: Industrial cities became less crowded as immigration increased.
choices.C: Municipal services expanded faster than urban population growth in every city.
choices.D: Foreign-born workers avoided factory and service employment.

questions.correctAnswerText: Immigration and industrial job growth contributed to rapid urbanization and pressure on city services.
questions.explanation: The table links immigration, jobs, density, and strained services in industrial cities.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of urban and immigrant change: foreign-born population in major industrial cities grew, tenement density rose, factory and service jobs expanded, and municipal services were strained. Which social development most directly accompanied these trends?

choices.A: The disappearance of ethnic neighborhoods
choices.B: The end of urban poverty
choices.C: The decline of political machines
choices.D: The growth of settlement houses and urban reform efforts

questions.correctAnswerText: The growth of settlement houses and urban reform efforts
questions.explanation: Urban crowding, poverty, and immigrant needs encouraged settlement houses and reform campaigns.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of urban and immigrant change: foreign-born population in major industrial cities grew, tenement density rose, factory and service jobs expanded, and municipal services were strained. Which political development was most directly encouraged by the conditions shown?

choices.A: The collapse of all city governments
choices.B: The abolition of citizenship for immigrants
choices.C: The rise of political machines that exchanged services and jobs for votes
choices.D: The immediate end of public health concerns in cities

questions.correctAnswerText: The rise of political machines that exchanged services and jobs for votes
questions.explanation: Strained services and immigrant urban communities created opportunities for political machines.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of urban and immigrant change: foreign-born population in major industrial cities grew, tenement density rose, factory and service jobs expanded, and municipal services were strained. Which broader economic context best explains the growth in factory and service jobs?

choices.A: The decline of mechanized production after the Civil War
choices.B: The expansion of industrial capitalism and urban markets
choices.C: The return to household production in most cities
choices.D: The collapse of railroad networks before 1870

questions.correctAnswerText: The expansion of industrial capitalism and urban markets
questions.explanation: Industrial capitalism increased demand for factory and service labor in growing cities.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated farmers’ alliance platform, 1890s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “The farmer sells in a market he cannot control and borrows at rates he cannot endure. Rail charges, tight money, and distant corporations press upon those who feed the nation.” Which movement is most directly reflected in the platform?

choices.A: Populism and agrarian protest against corporate and financial power
choices.B: The First Great Awakening
choices.C: The Federalist campaign for ratification
choices.D: The Harlem Renaissance

questions.correctAnswerText: Populism and agrarian protest against corporate and financial power
questions.explanation: The platform’s criticism of railroads, tight money, and corporations reflects Populist agrarian protest.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated farmers’ alliance platform, 1890s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The platform criticizes rail charges, tight money, and corporations. Which audience was the platform most likely intended to mobilize?

choices.A: British officials enforcing the Navigation Acts
choices.B: Northern abolitionists during the 1830s
choices.C: Suburban homeowners after World War II
choices.D: Farmers and workers dissatisfied with Gilded Age economic power

questions.correctAnswerText: Farmers and workers dissatisfied with Gilded Age economic power
questions.explanation: The platform sought to mobilize those who felt harmed by railroads, creditors, and corporate concentration.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated farmers’ alliance platform, 1890s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The platform argues that farmers were pressured by railroads, credit, and distant corporations. Which demand was most closely associated with this movement?

choices.A: Immediate U.S. entry into the League of Nations
choices.B: Expansion of plantation slavery into Kansas
choices.C: Regulation of railroads and changes to monetary policy
choices.D: Restoration of British imperial authority

questions.correctAnswerText: Regulation of railroads and changes to monetary policy
questions.explanation: Populists demanded railroad regulation and monetary reforms such as expanded currency.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, c. 1898–1905. The map highlights Puerto Rico, Guam, the Philippines, Hawaii, and naval routes across the Caribbean and Pacific. A note states that U.S. officials increasingly discussed coaling stations, overseas markets, and strategic reach. Which conclusion is best supported by the map?

choices.A: The United States withdrew from overseas affairs after the Civil War.
choices.B: The United States expanded its imperial and strategic presence beyond the continent.
choices.C: The United States limited foreign policy to alliances with Native nations.
choices.D: The United States rejected naval power as a foreign-policy tool.

questions.correctAnswerText: The United States expanded its imperial and strategic presence beyond the continent.
questions.explanation: The map’s territories and naval routes show overseas expansion in the Caribbean and Pacific.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, c. 1898–1905. Puerto Rico, Guam, the Philippines, Hawaii, and naval routes are highlighted, with notes about coaling stations, overseas markets, and strategic reach. Which debate was most directly intensified by the developments shown?

choices.A: Whether overseas empire was compatible with American republican ideals
choices.B: Whether colonial assemblies should resist the Stamp Act
choices.C: Whether the Articles of Confederation should be replaced
choices.D: Whether enslaved people should be counted for representation

questions.correctAnswerText: Whether overseas empire was compatible with American republican ideals
questions.explanation: Imperial expansion sparked debate over whether colonial rule contradicted American self-government principles.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, c. 1898–1905. The map highlights U.S. territories and naval routes across the Caribbean and Pacific, with notes about overseas markets and strategic reach. Which economic context helped encourage such expansion?

choices.A: The collapse of industrial production in the United States
choices.B: The end of international trade after Reconstruction
choices.C: The disappearance of U.S. interest in Asian markets
choices.D: Industrial growth increased interest in foreign markets and resources.

questions.correctAnswerText: Industrial growth increased interest in foreign markets and resources.
questions.explanation: Industrialization encouraged U.S. leaders and businesses to seek markets, resources, and strategic routes abroad.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described map, c. 1898–1905. The map highlights Puerto Rico, Guam, the Philippines, Hawaii, and naval routes across the Caribbean and Pacific. Which event most directly contributed to the territorial pattern shown?

choices.A: The War of 1812
choices.B: The Mexican-American War
choices.C: The Spanish-American War
choices.D: The Korean War

questions.correctAnswerText: The Spanish-American War
questions.explanation: The war and its aftermath led to U.S. control over Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Philippines.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1930s. A farm family stands beside a truck loaded with furniture, bedding, and cooking pots. Dust covers the road behind them. A handwritten sign on the truck reads “California.” Which development is best reflected in the photograph?

choices.A: Environmental disaster and economic hardship contributed to internal migration during the Great Depression.
choices.B: The New Deal ended all farm foreclosures before migration began.
choices.C: Industrial cities lost population because workers moved to isolated farms.
choices.D: World War II defense jobs caused immediate migration to California in 1930.

questions.correctAnswerText: Environmental disaster and economic hardship contributed to internal migration during the Great Depression.
questions.explanation: Dust, household goods, and a California destination indicate Dust Bowl displacement during the Depression.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1930s. A farm family stands beside a truck loaded with household goods. Dust covers the road, and a sign on the truck reads “California.” The photographer’s likely purpose was to document which issue?

choices.A: The success of plantation agriculture after the Civil War
choices.B: The human impact of economic depression and Dust Bowl displacement
choices.C: The growth of suburban highways after World War II
choices.D: The expansion of colonial towns in New England

questions.correctAnswerText: The human impact of economic depression and Dust Bowl displacement
questions.explanation: The photograph documents people affected by environmental disaster and economic collapse.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1930s. A Dust Bowl farm family travels west with household goods loaded onto a truck. Which federal response belongs to the same historical context?

choices.A: The Northwest Ordinance
choices.B: The Freedmen’s Bureau
choices.C: The Marshall Plan
choices.D: New Deal agricultural and relief programs

questions.correctAnswerText: New Deal agricultural and relief programs
questions.explanation: Dust Bowl migration and farm hardship occurred in the context of New Deal relief and agricultural policy.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Described photograph, 1930s. A displaced farm family travels west with household goods during the Dust Bowl. Which broader consequence followed from similar migrations?

choices.A: The complete disappearance of rural poverty
choices.B: The end of federal involvement in economic relief
choices.C: Strains in receiving communities and new attention to migrant labor conditions
choices.D: The immediate abolition of agricultural wage work

questions.correctAnswerText: Strains in receiving communities and new attention to migrant labor conditions
questions.explanation: Dust Bowl migration created labor competition, social tensions, and concern over migrant living and working conditions.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated policy memo, 1947, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “Aid to unstable nations should not be charity alone. It must show that democratic governments can answer hunger, fear, and Communist pressure without surrendering independence.” Which policy is most directly reflected in the memo?

choices.A: Containment through economic and military assistance
choices.B: Isolationism after World War I
choices.C: Imperial expansion after the Spanish-American War
choices.D: Neutrality during the French Revolution

questions.correctAnswerText: Containment through economic and military assistance
questions.explanation: The memo’s aid to democratic governments resisting Communism reflects containment policy.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated policy memo, 1947, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The memo argues that aid should help democratic governments resist “Communist pressure.” Which development most directly followed from this approach?

choices.A: The United States ended all overseas alliances.
choices.B: The federal government abolished national security agencies.
choices.C: The United States returned to pre-1890 isolationism.
choices.D: The United States expanded its global commitments during the Cold War.

questions.correctAnswerText: The United States expanded its global commitments during the Cold War.
questions.explanation: Containment led the United States to assume long-term diplomatic, economic, and military commitments abroad.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated policy memo, 1947, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The memo links foreign aid, democracy, and resistance to Communism. Which domestic development was connected to this Cold War context?

choices.A: The immediate end of anti-Communist investigations
choices.B: Loyalty programs and fears of Communist influence within the United States
choices.C: The disappearance of political debate over foreign policy
choices.D: The repeal of the Bill of Rights

questions.correctAnswerText: Loyalty programs and fears of Communist influence within the United States
questions.explanation: Cold War anti-Communism shaped domestic politics through investigations, loyalty programs, and public fears.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated policy memo, 1947, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The memo claims that aid can help democratic governments resist Communist pressure. Which argument is best supported by the memo?

choices.A: Cold War policy relied only on direct military conquest.
choices.B: The United States rejected all economic tools in foreign policy.
choices.C: Containment combined ideological, economic, and strategic goals.
choices.D: The United States treated Communism as only a domestic issue.

questions.correctAnswerText: Containment combined ideological, economic, and strategic goals.
questions.explanation: The memo connects democracy, aid, hunger, fear, and Communist pressure, showing containment’s multiple dimensions.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated statement from a Chicana labor organizer, early 1970s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. “We ask for more than wages. We ask that growers, lawmakers, and consumers see the people who harvest food as families with language, health, dignity, and rights.” The statement’s point of view is best explained by which historical situation?

choices.A: Latino/a labor and civil rights activism challenged economic exploitation and cultural marginalization.
choices.B: The First Great Awakening encouraged Puritan town meetings.
choices.C: The Kansas-Nebraska Act created popular sovereignty.
choices.D: The Articles of Confederation established a strong federal judiciary.

questions.correctAnswerText: Latino/a labor and civil rights activism challenged economic exploitation and cultural marginalization.
questions.explanation: The statement links labor, language, health, dignity, and rights, reflecting Chicano/a and farmworker activism.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated statement from a Chicana labor organizer, early 1970s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The speaker asks that consumers recognize the people who harvest food as families with dignity and rights. Which labor issue is most directly reflected?

choices.A: Railroad rate regulation in the Gilded Age
choices.B: Factory discipline in antebellum textile mills
choices.C: Unionization of steelworkers during World War II
choices.D: Farmworker organizing over wages, working conditions, and recognition

questions.correctAnswerText: Farmworker organizing over wages, working conditions, and recognition
questions.explanation: The speaker’s focus on growers, consumers, harvest labor, dignity, and rights points to farmworker organizing.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Original simulated statement from a Chicana labor organizer, early 1970s, based on historical context, not an actual quotation. The statement links labor rights, language, health, dignity, and family life. Which broader pattern does the statement reflect?

choices.A: The decline of rights-based activism after World War II
choices.B: The expansion of social movements that connected identity, labor, and political rights
choices.C: The complete absence of women from labor organizing
choices.D: The end of agricultural labor conflict after 1945

questions.correctAnswerText: The expansion of social movements that connected identity, labor, and political rights
questions.explanation: The statement reflects a broader postwar pattern of rights-based activism connecting identity and economic justice.

questions.prompt:
Stimulus: Plain-text table of political and social trends.
Trend | Late 20th to early 21st century pattern
Party voting in Congress | More ideologically divided
Cable news and digital media | More fragmented audiences
Religious and cultural issues | More prominent in national campaigns
Campaign fundraising | Increasingly expensive and nationalized
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Political parties became less ideologically distinct after 1980.
choices.B: Mass media fragmentation eliminated national political conflict.
choices.C: Political polarization grew alongside changes in media, campaign finance, and cultural politics.
choices.D: Religious and cultural issues disappeared from electoral politics.

questions.correctAnswerText: Political polarization grew alongside changes in media, campaign finance, and cultural politics.
questions.explanation: The table links ideological party voting, fragmented media, cultural issues, and nationalized fundraising.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of political and social trends: party voting becomes more ideologically divided, media audiences fragment, cultural issues become prominent in campaigns, and fundraising becomes more expensive and nationalized. Which development most directly contributed to the cultural pattern shown?

choices.A: Debates over abortion, school prayer, gender roles, and LGBTQ+ rights
choices.B: The disappearance of religious activism from politics
choices.C: The end of political campaigning after 1980
choices.D: The decline of television and internet media in public life

questions.correctAnswerText: Debates over abortion, school prayer, gender roles, and LGBTQ+ rights
questions.explanation: These issues became central to late twentieth- and early twenty-first-century cultural politics.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of political and social trends: party voting becomes more ideologically divided, media audiences fragment, cultural issues become prominent in campaigns, and fundraising becomes more expensive and nationalized. Which technological change helped intensify the media pattern shown?

choices.A: The invention of the cotton gin
choices.B: The expansion of canals and turnpikes
choices.C: The spread of telegraph lines in the 1840s
choices.D: The growth of cable television, talk radio, and internet platforms

questions.correctAnswerText: The growth of cable television, talk radio, and internet platforms
questions.explanation: Cable, talk radio, and online platforms contributed to fragmented political media audiences.

questions.prompt: Stimulus: Plain-text table of political and social trends: party voting becomes more ideologically divided, media audiences fragment, cultural issues become prominent in campaigns, and fundraising becomes more expensive and nationalized. Which argument about modern U.S. politics is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Polarization has been shaped by institutional, cultural, technological, and financial changes.
choices.B: Modern political conflict has been caused only by foreign policy debates.
choices.C: Party divisions disappeared as campaign fundraising increased.
choices.D: Digital media created a single shared national political culture.

questions.correctAnswerText: Polarization has been shaped by institutional, cultural, technological, and financial changes.
questions.explanation: The table supports a broad argument connecting polarization to party voting, media, cultural conflict, and campaign finance.
